original_abstract: Within the domain of image processing, a wide array of methodologies is dedicated to tasks including denoising, enhancement, segmentation, feature extraction, and classifica- tion. These techniques collectively address the challenges and opportunities posed by dif- ferent aspects of image analysis and manipulation, enabling applications across various fields. Each of these methodologies contributes to refining our understanding of images, extracting essential information, and making informed decisions based on visual data. Traditional image processing methods and Deep Learning (DL) models represent two dis- tinct approaches to tackling image analysis tasks. Traditional methods often rely on hand- crafted algorithms and heuristics, involving a series of predefined steps to process images. DL models learn feature representations directly from data, allowing them to automati- cally extract intricate features that traditional methods might miss. In denoising, techniques like Self2Self NN, Denoising CNNs, DFT-Net, and MPR-CNN stand out, offering reduced noise while grappling with challenges of data augmentation and parameter tuning. Image enhancement, facilitated by approaches such as R2R and LE-net, showcases potential for refining visual quality, though complexities in real-world scenes and authenticity persist. Segmentation techniques, including PSPNet and Mask-RCNN, exhibit precision in object isolation, while handling complexities like overlapping objects and robustness concerns. For feature extraction, methods like CNN and HLF-DIP showcase the role of automated recognition in uncovering image attributes, with trade-offs in interpretability and com- plexity. Classification techniques span from Residual Networks to CNN-LSTM, spotlight- ing their potential in precise categorization despite challenges in computational demands and interpretability. This review offers a comprehensive understanding of the strengths and limitations across methodologies, pavi
humanized_abstract: Image processing has become ubiquitous across various disciplines, and hence several methods are developed to deal with problems like removing noise, image restoration, contrast adjustment, feature extraction, image classification, etc. Many of these image processing techniques enhance understanding of images, feature extraction, etc., and decision making using image data. In all, image processing methods have been categorized mainly into two streams: traditional image processing and Deep Learning (DL) methods. Traditional methods always depend on manually created techniques and set of rules or algorithms where as Deep Learning based methods learn image features from data provided. While processing images using traditional image processing and Deep Learning based techniques like Self2Self NN, Denoising CNNs, DFT-Net, MPR-CNN are dealing in removing noise from image while on the other hand dealing with insufficient data set, selecting optimised number of parameters and various augmentations. On the enhancement part, models like R2R, LE-net, etc., can deal with the enhancement of quality of the given image. But as discussed earlier the authenticity and exactness of images are yet to be obtained. Furthermore, several segmentation methods were introduced for segmenting the images accurately such as, PSPNet and Mask-RCNN, but dealing with objects having overlapping structures and robustness of the model towards diverse noises still remain unsolved. Likewise, features in an image can also be extracted automatically using certain image processing and Deep Learning techniques which preserve the inner attributes of images like colors, etc. Hence features in images like CNN, HLF-DIP can be extracted, but preserving interpretability of the feature extraction models in Deep Learning and unavoidable complexities in traditional methods are required to be solved. Lastly, for the image classification models such as, Residual Networks, and CNN-LSTM can classify images into certain predefined classes efficiently. However, Deep Learning models required high computational powers for image processing, which is to be addressed to a large extent by applying efficient coding methods. Similarly, understanding of the classified classes for DL based methods is to be determined so that their inner attributes may also be extracted and utilized.
